description: Please comment if I'm wrong about the solution. ----- Huge thanks to totoro and manwich18 for correcting me.
sgf:
(;C[What is the minimum number of stones (of the same color) needed to form a group that is alive as it stands? Suppose that no more moves of the group's color is allowed to be played afterwards.FORCE]LB[dj:4]LB[gj:6]LB[jj:8]LB[mj:10]LB[pj:12]AP[goproblems]
(;B[gj];W[hj]C[Correct. Now, how many unique shapes are there that uses 6 stones to make a group that's alive (no matter what other than filling in the eyes)?FORCE]LB[dp:2]LB[gp:3]LB[jp:4]LB[mp:5]LB[pp:6]
(;B[jp]C[]
(;W[kp]C[Correct. These are the shapes.  RIGHT]AB[ar]AB[bs]AB[cr]AB[bq]AB[aq]AB[cs]AB[ab]AB[ba]AB[bc]AB[cb]AB[ca]AB[cc]AB[rb]AB[sb]AB[ra]AB[rc]AB[rd]AB[sd]AB[ss]AB[rr]AB[qs]AB[qr]AB[sq]AB[rq])
(;W[as];B[ja]))
(;B[dp])
(;B[gp])
(;B[mp])
(;B[pp]))
